Re: How to find out the size of the wrote data (Nigel Cunningham)    9. Re: How to find out the size of the wrote data (Florian Strunk) ---------------------------------------------------------------------- >From mxwl@cmu.edu  Sat Apr 26 15:49:24 2008 Message: 1 Date: Sun, 24 Feb 2008 13:19:26 -0500 From: Jeremy Maitin-Shepard  Subject: Re: [TuxOnIce-users] What is the difference between S4 and 	S5? To: zixdtcx9-umyfr@lists.tuxonice.net Message-ID: <87y79ausi9.fsf@jbms.ath.cx> Content-Type: text/plain David Abrahams  writes: > on Sat Feb 23 2008, Nigel Cunningham  wrote: >> Hi. >>  >> David Abrahams wrote: >>> From all the documentation I can find, the *only* difference between s4  >>> and s5 is that in s4, an image of RAM has been written to disk.  But of  >>> course that's what TOI is all about.  So what is the difference between  >>> PowerDownMethod 4 and PowerDownMethod 5 when used with TOI?  I mean,  >>> even if I set it to 5, I still have the RAM image on disk... right? >>  >> S4 and S5 are ACPI states, reflected in the 4 and 5 for the powerdown  >> method value. ACPI state S4 is a state in which the BIOS knows that  >> there is an image on disk, and in which (post-resume), ACPI _WAK (IIRC)  >> methods are run which might make things work that otherwise wouldn't  >> properly work post-resume. >>  >> ACPI state S5 is a simple power off as far as ACPI and the BIOS are  >> concerned. When you're resuming, no ACPI _WAK methods are run. We're  >> purely dependent upon TuxOnIce and the OS driver suspend and resume  >> routines doing the right thing (of course that does still include ACPI  >> to some extent). >>  >> So, the normal thing ought to be to use PowerDownMethod 4. Historically,  >> though, that hasn't always worked for people. > Both seem to work OK for me... although it seems as though I might've > achieved success sooner had I known that S4 was worth trying, since it's > supposed to make more things work properly.  Shouldn't the docs, HOWTOs, > and default suspend2.conf be updated to use s4 as the default and tell > people to try s5 if it doesn't work? In theory, I believe there is only one reason why S4 is useful, namely if the system supports certain wakeup devices for S4 that are not supported for S5, and you'd like to use them. In practice, though, with the current Linux hibernate implementations (both Tuxonice and swsusp), neither the hibernate nor the resume paths are exactly what the ACPI spec. envisions from the point of view of the devices (and furthermore, many devices and BIOS implementations may not even be exactly to spec.), and as a result the different steps done if S4 is used rather than just plain powerdown can sometimes help.  Note that I am specifically thinking of how the "resume" kernel typically initializes ACPI once prior to loading the saved image, and then the saved image attempts to reinitialize ACPI again, and also how devices are repeatedly placed in, and then taken out of, low power states suitable for S3 in the process of entering hibernation, and then on resume from hibernation, the "resume" driver function is called, because with the current interfaces this is the only thing that is available, but this may not know to do the appropriate thing since it is also used to resume the devices from S3. --  Jeremy Maitin-Shepard ------------------------------ >From nlnpr@nigel.suspend2.net  Sat Apr 26 15:49:24 2008 Message: 2 Date: Mon, 25 Feb 2008 07:57:45 +1100 From: Nigel Cunningham  Subject: Re: [TuxOnIce-users] What is the difference between S4 and 	S5? To: TuxOnIce users' list  Cc: zixdtcx9-umyfr@lists.tuxonice.net Message-ID: <47C1DA49.9060503@nigel.suspend2.net> Content-Type: text/plain; charset=ISO-8859-1; format=flowed Hi. Jeremy Maitin-Shepard wrote: > David Abrahams  writes: >  >> on Sat Feb 23 2008, Nigel Cunningham  wrote: >>> Hi. >>> >>> David Abrahams wrote: >>>> From all the documentation I can find, the *only* difference between s4  >>>> and s5 is that in s4, an image of RAM has been written to disk.  But of  >>>> course that's what TOI is all about.  So what is the difference between  >>>> PowerDownMethod 4 and PowerDownMethod 5 when used with TOI?  I mean,  >>>> even if I set it to 5, I still have the RAM image on disk... right? >>> S4 and S5 are ACPI states, reflected in the 4 and 5 for the powerdown  >>> method value. ACPI state S4 is a state in which the BIOS knows that  >>> there is an image on disk, and in which (post-resume), ACPI _WAK (IIRC)  >>> methods are run which might make things work that otherwise wouldn't  >>> properly work post-resume. >>> >>> ACPI state S5 is a simple power off as far as ACPI and the BIOS are  >>> concerned. When you're resuming, no ACPI _WAK methods are run. We're  >>> purely dependent upon TuxOnIce and the OS driver suspend and resume  >>> routines doing the right thing (of course that does still include ACPI  >>> to some extent). >>> >>> So, the normal thing ought to be to use PowerDownMethod 4. Historically,  >>> though, that hasn't always worked for people. >  >> Both seem to work OK for me... although it seems as though I might've >> achieved success sooner had I known that S4 was worth trying, since it's >> supposed to make more things work properly.  Shouldn't the docs, HOWTOs, >> and default suspend2.conf be updated to use s4 as the default and tell >> people to try s5 if it doesn't work? >  > In theory, I believe there is only one reason why S4 is useful, namely > if the system supports certain wakeup devices for S4 that are not > supported for S5, and you'd like to use them. >  > In practice, though, with the current Linux hibernate implementations > (both Tuxonice and swsusp), neither the hibernate nor the resume paths > are exactly what the ACPI spec. envisions from the point of view of the > devices (and furthermore, many devices and BIOS implementations may not > even be exactly to spec.), and as a result the different steps done if > S4 is used rather than just plain powerdown can sometimes help.  Note > that I am specifically thinking of how the "resume" kernel typically > initializes ACPI once prior to loading the saved image, and then the > saved image attempts to reinitialize ACPI again, and also how devices > are repeatedly placed in, and then taken out of, low power states > suitable for S3 in the process of entering hibernation, and then on > resume from hibernation, the "resume" driver function is called, because > with the current interfaces this is the only thing that is available, > but this may not know to do the appropriate thing since it is also used > to resume the devices from S3. Yeah. That is changing in 2.6.25. Regards, Nigel ------------------------------

subject: Re: [Python-Dev] how to easily consume just the parts of eggs that	are good for you
body:
At 12:12 AM 4/10/2008 -0700, Stephen Hansen wrote:
>I think PJE's idea here is very good. Just include certain files and 
>such in the RPM/DEB that will satisfy the 
>"python-package-management" system. For RPM/DEB users and their OS's 
>database of packages, its irrelevant largely-- they'll still keep 
>using their own system. But if a product needs something without a 
>.deb or .rpm, or if someone's on an operating system without a 
>native system-- they can still gather everything they need.

I've narrowed it a bit from that, actually.  It's safest if 
easy_install simply refuses to touch any files that it can't tell 
were installed by it (or a compatible system, eg. distutils.)

While that won't solve Paul Moore's desire for the One True Package 
Manager, it will at least make it possible to move forward.

subject: [UAI] [CFP] The First CLIMA Contest
body:
[we apologize for multiple copies of this e-mail]


		*** The First CLIMA Contest ***

Organised by JÃ¼rgen Dix, Technical University of Clausthal, and Mehdi
Dastani, Utrecht University

http://clima.deis.unibo.it/contest.html

Multi-agent systems are beginning to play an important role in todayâs
software development. See for example the forthcoming new International
Journal of Agent-Oriented Software Engineering

This competition is an attempt to stimulate research in the area of
multi-agent systems by

    * identifying key problems and
    * collecting suitable benchmarks

that can serve as milestones for testing new approaches and techniques
from computational logics. While there exist several competitions in
various parts of artificial intelligence (theorem proving, planning,
robo-cup etc) and, lately, also in specialised areas in agent systems
(trading agents), the emphasis of this contest is on the use of
'computational logic' in (multi-) agent systems.

We expect to promote the development of multi-agent systems by first
identifying difficult problems and then finding solutions by comparing
different approaches from computational logic for solving them. While
this idea seems very appealing, it is not an easy task to come up with a
particular scenario that serves as a basis for a contest. Such a
scenario should be generic enough to be applicable for a wide range of
techniques of computational logic, but it should also be precise enough
so that different approaches can be tested and compared against each
other.


* Scenario description

This competition is organised as part of CLIMA and consists of
developing multi-agent systems to solve a cooperative task in a
dynamically changing environment. The environment of the multi-agent
system is a grid-like world where agents can move from one slot to a
neighbouring slot if there is no agent already in that slot. In this
environment, food can appear in all but one of these slots. The special
slot, in which no food can appear, is considered as a depot where the
agents can bring and collect their food. An agent can observe if there
is food in the slot it is currently visiting. Initially, food can be
placed in some randomly selected slots. During the execution, additional
food can appear dynamically in randomly selected slots except the depot
slot. The agents may have/play different roles (such as explorer or
collector), communicate and cooperate in order to find and collect food
in an efficient and effective way.

We encourage submissions that specify and design a multi-agent system in
terms of high-level concepts such as goals, beliefs, plans, roles,
communication, coordination, negotiation, and dialogue in order to
generate an efficient and effective solution for the above mentioned
application. Moreover, the multi-agent system implementations should be
based on computational logic techniques (e.g. logic programming, formal
calculi, etc) and they should reflect their design in a direct and
intuitive way.

The challenge of this competition is thus to use computational logic
techniques to provide implemented models for the abstract concepts that
are used in the specification and design of multi-agent systems. These
implemented models should be integrated to implement the above-mentioned
application intuitively, directly, and effectively.
Submission format


* A submission consists of two parts.

The first part is a description of analysis, design and implementation
of a multi-agent system for the above application. Existing multi-agent
system methodologies such as Gaia, Prometheus and Tropos can be used
(not demanded) to describe the analysis and design of the system. For
the description of the implementation, it should be explained how the
design is implemented. This can be done by explaining, for example,
which computational logic techniques are used to implement certain
aspects of the multi-agent system (including issues related to
individual agents). The maximum length of this description is 5 pages
according to the LNCS format.

The second part is an (executable) implementation of the application. We
do not demand any particular way (data format, algorithm, mechanism) to
implement the system as long as it is implemented as a multi-agent
system and as long as the environment is a 20x20 grid. Moreover, it
should be possible to configure the initial state of the environment to
place food in arbitrary slots. During the execution food should appear
automatically every 20 seconds in a randomly selected slot. The
multi-agent system will be run with 4 agents that are positioned
initially at the corners of the grid. The implementation should be
executable on a windows or linux machine.
How To Submit

Please follow this link to register and submit the 5 page description of
your solution. You can then submit the implementation by e-mail (to the
CLIMA Contest Chairs) or by specifying in your paper a URL where the
implementation can be downloaded from. In the registration, please
select 'Competition' in the paper type (tracks) drop-down menu.
Winning Criteria

The criteria that will be used to evaluate submission and to select the
first three winners are as follows:

   1. Original, innovative, and effective application of computational
logic techniques in solving specific multi-agent issues identified in
this application.
   2. The performance of the executable implementation. The performance
of the executable implementation will be measured based on the amount of
food that is collected by the multi-agent system in a certain period of
time. All programs will be run on the same machine (Windows/Linux double
boot machine).
   3. The quality of the description of analysis, design and
implementation of the multi-agent system, the elegance of its design and
implementation, and the ease of installation and execution of the
program.

Evaluation Committee

    * Marco Alberti, University of Ferrara, Italy
    * Federico Chesani, University of Bologna, Italy
    * Mehdi Dastani, Utrecht University, The Netherlands (chair)
    * JÃ¼rgen Dix, Technical University of Clausthal, Germany (chair)
    * Marco Gavanelli, University of Ferrara, Italy
    * Kostas Stathis, City University London, UK
    * Francesca Toni, Imperial College London, UK
    * Paolo Torroni, University of Bologna, Italy

Important Dates (no extensions)

    * Submission: May 20, 2005
    * Notification: May 27, 2005
    * Camera-Ready: June 6, 2005
    * Winner announcement: at CLIMA, June 27-29, 2005

Prize

There will be a prize for the winner. A selection of teams will be
invited to extend their description for publication in the
post-proceedings.

subject: [UAI] Postdoc position
body:
Postdoc position at the Machine Intelligence Group, Aalborg University, 
Denmark.

A postdoc position is avaliable at the Department of Computer Science, 
the MI group, from August 1st 2005 or at the most convenient time 
thereafter. The position is for one year with the possibility of renewal 
for further two years. It is a combined research and teaching position. 
As Aalborg University is a "problem based learning" institution, the 
teaching will mainly consist of supervision of student projects 
(undergraduates as well as graduates), and profficiency in English is 
required.

We look for a candidate with qualifications within datamining, 
intelligent agents in computer games, or graphical models for decision 
support systems.

Further information on the MI-group can be found at 
www.cs.aau.dk/research/MI

Persons interested in the position are kindly requested to contact 
professor Finn V. Jensen, ejr@cs.aau.dk

subject: Re: [Python-Dev] [Distutils] how to easily consume just the parts	of eggs that are good for you
body:
Phillip J. Eby wrote:
> It would be, if .eggs were a packaging format, rather than a binary 
> distribution/runtime format.
>
> Remember "eggs are to Python as jars are to Java" -- a Java .jar 
> doesn't contain documentation either, unless it's needed at 
> runtime.  Same for configuration files.
>   
But there's generally no need to easily have a look into a .class file 
with a tool the user
is used to whereas for python, we're often interested in knowing the 
details. And having
a zip file in my way to the source has left me frustrated often enough.

If you want to be consequent and consistent leave out the .py files from 
eggs, a jar file
normally doesn't contain .java sources files either.

> They're not system packages, in other words.  The assumption that 
> they are is another marketing failure, due to conflation of "package 
> == distribution of python code" and "package == thing you manage with 
> a system packager".  People see, "I put my package in an .egg" and 
> think it's the latter definition, when it's barely even the former.  :)
>   
I agree that they are not system packages, but I would have prefered to 
install multiple versions
of a package to separate "site-packages" directories, something that is 
really well understood by
most unsofisticated python programmers. The selection of the version 
could then be made at
runtime by a PYTHONPATH setting and not by fiddling with .pth files 
(something that could
be autmated by a tool and persisted in batch files).

subject: Re: [ILUG] MySQL Database question
body: Hamilton, David (TSG C&I) wrote: > I am looking to create a MySQL database that can be moved around from system to system. >>From what I can see, when you create a database, a directory with that name is created under the data directory. > Is this all that is required to run the database? (Assuming the base config files are all the same) > I need to be able to take the DB on an external disk and reconnect it under the same mount point on another system and be able to start it up. > I'm coming at this from an Oracle perspective where every DB needs an init.ora file.... >  > Thanks, >         David. >  You can dump the entire contents of the database to an SQL file. MySQL comes with a utility called mysqldump so you would use is as follows: mysqldump database name > dump.sql The above works as the MySQL root user with no password. To do the same as another MySQL user with permissions on that database you would use: mysqldump -u user -p database name > /destination.sql You will then be prompted for the password. To import the dump you would use the following: mysql database < dump.sql Again using the -u and -p switches as needed. To dump the contents of a remote MySQL server database to your machine you would use: mysqldump -h hostname -u root -p database > dump.sql And to restore: mysql -h hostname -u root -p database < dump.sql Cheers Robert

subject: Re: [UAI] causality
body:
Hi Lotfi,

It's certainly true that our definition of causality depnds on the
causal structure.  In the same way, Tarski's definition of truth depends
on having a relational model.  It is also true that there are times when
it might be hard to construct an appropriate structural model, because
we don't know all the necessary details.  In the same way, it is
sometimes difficult to describe an appropriate relational model.
Moreover, people may often disagree as to which structural model best
describes a situation; they may disagree on both the facts of the matter
(what Judea and I call the context) and on the equations (roughtly
speaking, what causes what).  Indeed, I believe that lawyers often
implicitly argue these issues in court.   Judea and I viewed it as a
feature of our definition that it was dependeot on the model (as is 
Tarski's definition of truth).  We were well aware that people might
well argue as to what was the most appropriate model.

But all this does not mean that causality is undefinable, as far as I'm
concerned.  By analogy, if you tell me "John is Swedish" and then you
ask "Is it true that John's hair is red", I would say that you simply
haven't given me enough details to determine the truth of that sentence.
Now you could say that such details are difficult to obtain (John is
currently mountain climbing, and not accessible by email), and even that
people disagree on John's haircolor, but none of that doesn't mean that
"truth" is undefinable.  

I think it is quite possible to come up with useful definitions of
causality.  Moreover, the  definitions can guide us as to what
information we need to answer the questions you pose.  In your first
example, there are marketers and statisticians that believe they have
techniques to determine whether an increase in sales was caused by an
increase in the advertising budget.  But I think they would agree that
you didn't give them sufficient information to determine causality.

We may have to agree to disagree ...

Regards,
Joe

---------------------

Dear Joe:

    Many thanks for your clarifying comments. Your work and that of 
Judea Pearl are major contributions to the development of a better 
understanding of causality--a concept which has a position of centrality 
in science and, more particularly, in AI. But there is a sticky point in 
your theory--a point which is alluded to in the comment of Joseph Mitola 
III.

    Your definition of causality is conditioned on the existence of a 
structural model. The problem is that in most realistic 
settings--settings which are exemplified by Problems 1, 2 and 3--valid 
structural models are hard or impossible to construct. For example, in 
Problem 1, the increase in sales may be the result of increase in 
advertising, rainy weather, better economy, raising of prices by my 
competitors, etc. We have no model of how these factors combine to 
produce an increase in sales. The same applies to Problems 2 and 3.

    In the case of Problem 3, it should be noted that in some contexts 
causal strength may be equated to the degree of responsibility, but in 
most contexts they have different meanings. In Problem 3, the degrees of 
responsibility are not the same and cannot be assessed precisely. More 
specifically, the degree of responsibility of D would be high if D knew 
that his gun would be used to kill B, and it would be low if D had no 
idea that his gun is borrowed to kill someone. Assessment of degree of 
responsibility in social settings does not lend itself to scientific 
analysis; it is a matter of convention and is subject to debate.

    In my message of June 2nd, I made two statements: (a) causality is 
undefined; and (b) causality is undefinable. So far, I have not seen any 
definitions of causality that are applicable to Problems 1, 2 and 3. But 
why is causality undefinable?

    We all know that in mathematics there are many problems that have no 
solution. But what is widely unrecognized is that there are many 
concepts which intrinsically do not lend themselves to precise 
definition. An example is the concept of summary. Another example is the 
concept of relevance. In my view, the concept of causality falls into 
this category.

    In a general setting, the issue of definability and the related 
issue of precisiation are addressed in my papers "A Fuzzy-Algorithmic 
Approach to the Definition of Complex or Imprecise Concepts," /Int. 
Jour. Man-Machine Studies 8/, 249-291, 1976; "Causality is Indefinable - 
Towards a Theory of Hierarchical Definability," in: Intelligence in a 
Materials World: Selected Papers from IPMM-2001, The Third International 
Conference on Intelligent Processing and Manufacturing of Materials; 
Editors: J.A. Meech et al, CRC Press, Boca Raton, 29-34, 2002; 
"Precisiated Natural Language (PNL)," ** /AI Magazine/, Vol. 25, No. 3, 
74-91, 2004; and "Generalized Theory of Uncertainty--Principal Concepts 
and Ideas," to appear in Computational Statistics and Data Analysis.

    When we define a concept, C, we start with a perception of C. Then 
we precisiate our perception of C by describing its meaning in a 
definition language. If the definition language is a natural language, 
precisiation is referred to as mh-precisiation, with /m /standing for 
meaning and /h/ for human-oriented. If the definition language is a 
mathematical language, precisiation is referred to as mm-precisiation. 
Precisiation should be cointensive in the sense that mm-precisiation 
(meaning) of the definiens should be a good fit to the perception of C. 
Within this framework, what can be asserted is that it is not possible 
to construct a definition of causality--especially inverse 
causality--which is general, mm-precise and cointensive. A major 
obstacle as noted in my message is the problem of decombination.

       Warm regards,

             Lotfi
-- 
Lotfi A. Zadeh
Professor in the Graduate School
Director, Berkeley Initiative in Soft Computing (BISC)

subject: Re: Score vs Bars
body:
Theo Van Dinter wrote:
> On Wed, Feb 06, 2008 at 11:52:09PM -0500, Don Ireland wrote:
>   
>> When I look at the message source, the following is among them--this is 
>> actually from YOUR reply.  
>>
>> X-Spam-Status: No, score=-6.6
>> X-Spam-Score: -65
>> X-Spam-Bar: ------
>>     
>
> In a standard config, X-Spam-Status has a lot more information, and
> X-Spam-Score and X-Spam-Bar don't exist.  You must have a local configuration
> (or a third-party utility which does its own markup,) which has these setup.Loo
>
>   
Looks like it might have been generated by whatever these guys are using:

http://mail-archives.apache.org/mod_mbox/spamassassin-users/200708.mbox/%4n283494750276.11412.i_hcxmn@012.net.il%3E

Looks like there's some kind of exim setup that does this..

Based on that I can make these guesses:

 X-Spam-Score seems to be score *10, and truncated instead of rounded. 
This accounts for how -6.6 can become -65.. (-6.58 will round to -6.6, 
but multiply and truncate and you get -65)

X-Spam-Bar probably generates a series of - or + signs for each full 
point of score in that direction. (similar to SA's _STARS(*)_ tag, but 
with negative scores represented). It's a wild guess, but seems reasonable.

were this not true, there might have been -varying opinions as to the advisability in the choice of the time for publicationUp to then I had heard nothing about it, BISMARCK cither from the Prince or the Foreign Office, although it happened that I had concerned myself especially with Russian mattersDuring the ninety-nine days, while I was still Crown Prince, I had silently concerned myself with those personages to whom I proposed later to give appointments, since the physicians had left me no doubt that my father had only a short time to liveFrom all sides  BISMARCK came calls for troops ; every mine owner wanted, if possible, to have sentries posted outside his roomHe also had travel arrangements drawn up for meBut my reverence for Bismarck, the great statesman, remained unalteredI did away with the term maison militaire and transformed it into Main Headquarters of His MajestyIn choosing my entourage I took the advice of only one man in whom I reposed special confidence, my former chief and brigade commander, Gen eral afterward Adjutant General von Versen, a man of straightforward, knightly, rather harsh character, an officer of the old Prussian school, a typical chip of the old blockNotwithstanding this, I had dealings with many important men of the party and managed to interest them in practical collaboration for the good of allI am objective enough to ask myself to what extent the courtesy of one ruler to another and possibly, in addition, the at the elimination of a statesman of Bismarck's importance, can have influenced the Tsar, con sciously or unconsciously, in making the above- mentioned statement Prince Bismarck's belief in the Tsar's trust in him was, subjectively, undoubt edly genuine; and, moreover, there can be no doubt as to the esteem in which Alexander III held Bismarck's ability as a statesman THE KAISER'S MEMOIRS The Foreign Office was conducted with the strictest discipline by Count Herbert, whose rude ness toward his employees particularly struck meI released this vol ume after I had sought and obtained my rightsIt should be borne in mind with what generation I grew up the generation of the devotees of Bis marckFor this very reason alone it is stupid to accuse me of not having recognized the greatness of Prince Bis marckMy deep grief and sor row were all the greater because it was almost impossible for me to speak alone with my beloved fatherOn the contrary! He was far too great a statesman to mistake the importance of the labor question to the stateBut both he and the country needed Bismarck too badly

Date: Wed, 20 Feb 2008 20:03:59 -0800 (PST) Message-ID: <15b26ef3-3e9f-42d4-a859-e087fb8fc955@o10g2000hsf.googlegroups.com> Mime-Version: 1.0 Content-Type: text/plain; charset=ISO-8859-1 Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit >  I have a feeling that I am going about this in the wrong > way.  Can I use hashes in a better way to sort the data based on the > keys?  Better yet, can I evaluate the number of keys that match each > other? I don't understand what that means. John Oh - I wanted to eliminate all members of the array that had more than 10 instances of the same port.  I was hoping that you could do something like "count keys where port = gi1/1/49".  After knowing how many gi1/1/49 there were, you could remove them if they met your high water mark. I ended out just looking for duplicates, which will achieve the basic task at hand.:    ### Sort the data based on the port name    @data = sort { $a-> {port} cmp $b-> {port} } @data;    ### Test to see if the port names are the same (look ahead and behind)    $j = 0;    for($i = 0; $i <= $#data; $i++) {       if ($j < $#data) {          $j = $i+1;          $k = $i-1;          #print "$data[$i]{'vlan'}, $data[$i]{'host'}, $data[$i] {'mac'}, $data[$i]{'port'}, i = $i, j= $j, k= $k, total = $#data\\n";          if ((($data[$i]{'port'} ne $data[$j]{'port'}) && ($data[$i] {'port'} ne $data[$k]{'port'})) || ($data[$i]{'port'} eq "wireless")) {          open (dataDump, ">>$data_dump") or die "Can't edit $data_dump : $!";             print dataDump ("$data[$i]{'vlan'}\\t$data[$i]{'host'}\\t $data[$i]{'mac'}\\t$data[$i]{'port'}\\n");          close (dataDump);          }       } else {          $k = $i-1;          if (($data[$i]{'port'} ne $data[$k]{'port'}) || ($data[$i] {'port'} eq "wireless")) {          #print "$data[$i]{'vlan'}, $data[$i]{'host'}, $data[$i] {'mac'}, $data[$i]{'port'}, i = $i, j= $j, k= $k, total = $#data\\n";          open (dataDump, ">>$data_dump") or die "Can't edit $data_dump : $!";             print dataDump ("$data[$i]{'vlan'}\\t$data[$i]{'host'}\\t $data[$i]{'mac'}\\t$data[$i]{'port'}\\n");          close (dataDump);          }       } Ultimately, I am trying to track down MAC addresses on a network and associate them with a port.  I build @data by reading in a screen scrap of every MAC address table on the network (one at a time), which is the output from a different script.  I need to eliminate MACs that are trunked across other devices (they show up as a bunch of MACs on a single interface, in several other mac-address tables on other devices).  By only looking for unique addresses, I can find anyone plugged into a legitimate port.  However, I cannot find someone that has plugged in a Linksys switch into a port - because multiple MACs will be associated with a single port, if they have more than one active device on their device.  I figure that 2 - 16 of these "bad" addresses could show up from a rogue switch.  So, I figured that I'd start at 10 and tweak it from there. I thought that all this information would confuse the issue that I am trying to resolve.  I hope that it is not an overload. Thank you all again for your assistance.  I will keep checking back in to see if anyone has a better idea to solve what I like to call the "real world issue", versus the "everyone behaves as I want them to" problem.  Even though this solves 90%+ of the problem, I want to clean up the solution a bit. My script :         # Duh         use Net::IMAP::Simple;         use Email::Simple;         # Create the object         my $imap = Net::IMAP::Simple->new('xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx') ||                 die "Unable to connect to IMAP:  $Net::IMAP::Simple::errstr\\n";         # Log on         if(!$imap->login('lto@xx.xxx','xxxxx')){                 print STDERR "Login failed: " . $imap->errstr . "\\n";                 exit(64);         }         my $nm = $imap->select('INBOX');         for(my $i = 1; $i <= $nm; $i++){                 my $test_sub = Email::Simple->new(join '', @{  $imap->top($i) } );                 my $subject = substr($test_sub->header('Subject'),0,25);                 if($subject=="xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"){                         my $es = $imap->get($i);                          ##################################################################                         # I want to use @{$es} and extract all  attachment from it and save them somewhere   #                                            ##################################################################                 }         }         $imap->quit; --anodpocnjgcdginpdpff-- >From wyz@clerew.man.ac.uk  Sun Feb 24 10:30:12 2008 Message: 1 Date: Wed, 13 Feb 2008 11:18:39 -0000 From: "Charles Lindsey"  Subject: Re: [ietf-dkim] Re: ISSUE: SSP-02: MX Record publishing 	mandate to	reduceDNS overhead for SSP Discovery and to detect 	fraudulent messages To: DKIM  Message-ID:  Content-Type: text/plain; format=flowed; delsp=yes; charset=iso-8859-1 On Tue, 12 Feb 2008 18:57:32 -0000, Douglas Otis    wrote: > On Feb 12, 2008, at 7:53 AM, Frank Ellermann wrote: > >> Douglas Otis wrote: >> >>> the SSP draft should mandate publishing MX records whenever an SSP   >>> record is also published. >> >> -1 >> >> SSP (or ASP) have no business to "mandate" MX records, that's not their   >> job.  MX records are not required for (2)821(bis) interoperability, and   >> RFC 2119 has a very clear policy about arbitrary MUSTard. > > The MUST only occurs in conjunction with publishing SSP records.  This   > does not mandate publishing of MX records when SSP is not used. Exactly. An SSP record is published using the DNS system. One of the   functions of the SSP draft is to specify the precise means of such   publication. So there is no reason why it should not say "An SSP record MUST NOT be   published unless there also exists a corresponfing MX record". So if   someone publishes one contary to that, then  it is not a valid SSP record,   and hence may be ignored by evaluators. I son't see any problem with doing that, provided of course that the   technical benefits warrant it. --  Charlesï¿½H.ï¿½Lindseyï¿½---------Atï¿½Home,ï¿½doingï¿½myï¿½ownï¿½thing------------------------ Tel:ï¿½+44ï¿½161ï¿½436ï¿½6131ï¿½                       ï¿½ï¿½ï¿½Web:ï¿½http://www.cs.man.ac.uk/~chl Email:ï¿½wyz@clerew.man.ac.ukï¿½ï¿½ï¿½ï¿½ï¿½ï¿½Snail:ï¿½5ï¿½Clerewoodï¿½Ave,ï¿½CHEADLE,ï¿½SK8ï¿½3JU,ï¿½U.K. PGP:ï¿½2C15F1A9ï¿½ï¿½ï¿½ï¿½ï¿½ï¿½Fingerprint:ï¿½73ï¿½6Dï¿½C2ï¿½51ï¿½93ï¿½A0ï¿½01ï¿½E7ï¿½65ï¿½E8ï¿½64ï¿½7Eï¿½14ï¿½A4ï¿½ABï¿½A5 ------------------------------

subject: [Ip-health] ACTION launches www.action.org!
body:
--
[ Picked text/plain from multipart/alternative ]
Dear Fellow Global Citizen,

I am very excited to announce the launch of ACTION's new website - www.action.org!

Visit this site frequently to discover:
    * New TB advocacy opportunities around the world.
    * Critical actions you can take on other global health and development issues.
    * Our TB advocacy presence at the International AIDS Conference in Mexico City.
    * A wealth of resources on how to be a more effective advocate.
    * New advocacy tips each week.
    * The latest TB news.
    * And much, much more!

The Advocacy to Control Tuberculosis Internationally (ACTION) project is an international partnership of advocates working to mobilize resources to treat and prevent the spread of tuberculosis (TB), a global disease that kills one person every 20 seconds.

ACTION uses cutting-edge advocacy strategies to support country-specific and global solutions for the control of TB.  Since its inception in 2004, ACTION partners have achieved numerous successes and made great progress towards eradicating TB, including:
    * Significantly contributing to the mobilization of more than US $554 million in additional funding for fighting TB from the project's target donor countries, and
    * Bringing together the TB and HIV/AIDS communities in the project's target high burden countries to improve their response to TB-HIV by setting bold policies and increasing civil society's advocacy capacity.

Yet, there is still much more work to be done and www.action.org is the place to do it! On our site, global citizens and experienced advocates like you can become more educated about tuberculosis and get involved in the fight against TB and other global health and development issues.

Visit www.action.org and I'm confident you will find new and effective ways to become further engaged in advocacy efforts to control TB globally.

Sincerely,

Joanne Carter
ACTION Project Leader
RESULTS Educational Fund Executive Director

ACTION is a project of ACTION India, Global Health Advocates, Global Health Advocates France, Kenya AIDS NGOs Consortium (KANCO), RESULTS Canada, RESULTS Educational Fund (US), RESULTS Japan, RESULTS UK

subject: Re: [FoRK] I'd love to do this, and I'm afraid to do this...
body:
On 11/13/07, Russell Turpin  wrote:
>
> For quite a while, I wished someone would come up with a financial records
> consolidation service. Nothing fancy. Just let me see my accounts from
> brokers, banks, etc., all in one place. I'm not too surprised that someone
> has:
>
> http://www.mint.com/
>
> The problem, of course, is that you have to give the site the username and
> password for all your accounts. Which means that if some hacker manages to
> break into their data, they can wipe you out. Literally. Completely.
>
> Which raises the interesting question: Why don't bank and broker websites
> support capabilities? A service such as this only needs to retrieve
> information, not the ability to manipulate the accounts. Better, of course,
> would be an architecture where such a service never had an account name and
> password, but retrieved the data from the various financial websites
> through a proxy server running on your own machine.
>
> Color me paranoid, but I don't think I'm yet ready to register with some
> site in the sky all my usernames and passwords at financial institutions.
> Yeah, it provides lots of convenience. But this phrase keeps repeating in
> my head: single point of failure. Single point of total failure.

agreed.

it does bring up the point that a *read-only* interface for common
sites would be nice though. of course it comes with it's own set of
security requirements [i.e. "read only" is only a concept; if you
don't validate your parameters it quickly becomes read/write :D]

perhaps some sort of well-thought-out secure language could help.
needs more thought. as a seed, though: caja.

http://www.links.org/?p=271 [i think maybe this was brought up on this
list recently].

-- 
mike
http://lets.coozi.com.au/

subject: [UAI] A new website for Graphical model's reasoning algorithms at UC-Irvine
body:
Dear Colleagues,

I am pleased to announce the release of the website "GraphModAlg@UCI",
Graphical Model Algorithms at UC Irvine. Its purpose is to make
available implementations of the algorithms our group develops for
reasoning in graphical models.

We provide various exact and approximate algorithms for Bayesian network
tasks as well as constraint networks and integer programming problems.
We also host a repository of example problem instances.

The website's URL is at http://graphmod.ics.uci.edu/

A list of the algorithms currently available:

* aolibPE computes the exact probability of evidence for Bayesian
networks using AND/OR search spaces.

* Bucket Elimination performs variable elimination to obtain the exact
probability of evidence.

* IJGP (Iterative Join Graph Propagation) computes approximate beliefs
in Bayesian networks using belief propagation algorithms.

* IJGPIS computes approximate beliefs and probability of evidence in
Bayesian networks using hybrid of importance sampling, Iterative Join 
Graph Propagation (IJGP) and Relational Consistency.

* IJGPSampleSearch computes approximate beliefs and probability of
evidence in Bayesian networks using hybrid of importance sampling, 
SampleSearch and Iterative Join Graph Propagation (IJGP).

* aolibMPE implements several exact AND/OR search algorithms to compute
the most probable explanation in Bayesian networks using Mini-Bucket
heuristics and caching.

* mbeMPE implements the Mini-Bucket approximation of the most probable
explanation in Bayesian networks.

* aolibWCSP finds the optimal solution to weighted constraint
satisfaction problems via various AND/OR search algorithms using
Mini-Bucket heuristics and caching.

* mbeWCSP implements the Mini-Bucket approximation for weighted
constraint satisfaction problems.

* aolibILP computes the optimal solution to 0/1 integer linear programs
using AND/OR search.

* treeDecomp computes a tree decomposition of a given graphical model.


Comments and feedback are most welcomed and should be sent to me 
(lojtdhx@ics.uci.edu) and/or Lars Otten (bdmqhm@ics.uci.edu)

Hope you will find this useful,
----Rina.

-- 
Rina Dechter                                    	 lojtdhx@ics.uci.edu
Bren School of Information and Computer Sciences   (949) 824-6556
University of California, Irvine                   fax: (949)-824-4056
Irvine, CA 92697-3425
http://www.ics.uci.edu/~dechter

subject: Autoresponder
body: Hi, Having given up on vacation because it just seems to not do anything, I have been trying to use the following to setup an autoresponder for a one user: http://workaround.org/moin/PostfixTutorialContributions Autoresponder Contributed by Bjoern Meier     * Use an entry in the forwardings table to forward email for a certain user to the email address ervv@domain.tld@autoreply.domain.tld          * Add an entry to the transport file that sends email for the domain autoreply.domain.tld to the autoreply: transport service:     autoreply.domain.tld autoreply:     * Add a service to the master.cf like this:       autoreply       unix    -       n       n       -       -       pipe         flags= user=mailer argv=/usr/local/bin/autoreply $sender $recipient     * Create an executable batch script in       /usr/local/bin/autoreply: mail -s"Zur Zeit im Urlaub" -a"From: <$2>" $1 < /etc/postfix/autoreply.txt         *  Put an autoreply text into the file /etc/postfix/autoreply.txt          * Reload Postfix The only thing I have changed is that the -a option is not supported by our mail command (Centos 4) so I have removed it. One thing I am not sure about is the first step - we are using a virtual file and so for the user I have a line like this: testuser	paaqpoda@domain.tld which I have changed to: testuser	paaqpoda@autoreply.domain.tld but the instruction above implies the destination is set to: paaqpoda@domain.tld@autoreply.domain.tld  or is this a typo?? I believe I have everything in place but the maillog always shows that emails to 'paaqpoda@autoreply.domain.tld' are being forwarded to our external mail relay, which then bounces them as undeliverable - it's as if the transport is being ignored (I have 'postmap'ed the edited file. Is there anyone who has this working (or tips on 'vacation'?). Thanks Nigel Kendrick >From ahlg.rwhsv@gmail.com  Sun Feb 24 10:31:21 2008 Message-ID: <58ce48dc0802181835k1b19db86rb48be48fa6f6eeda@mail.gmail.com> Date: Mon, 18 Feb 2008 21:35:43 -0500 From: "Chas. Owens"  To: "Gunnar Hjalmarsson"  Subject: Re: Dependancies Cc: soiofdvxg@perl.org MIME-Version: 1.0 Content-Type: text/plain; charset=ISO-8859-1 Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit On Feb 18, 2008 9:08 PM, Gunnar Hjalmarsson  wrote: > Chas. Owens wrote: > > On Feb 18, 2008 7:52 PM, Gunnar Hjalmarsson  wrote: > > snip > >>> eval "use SomeModule" if $ENV{USE_SOMEMODULE}; > >>> > >>> or the sort of tricks that the DBI and other factory classes use. > >> What would be the problem with that? > >> > >> $ perl -le 'eval "use Time::Local"; print for keys %INC' > > snip > > > > Please reflect on the lack of the if statement in your code. > > Sorry, but I still don't get it. > > $ perl -le 'eval "use Time::Local" if 1+1==2; print for keys %INC' snip That is not a valid example; it is always true.  My example used an environmental variable that may or may not be true.  The code to detect what dependencies the code has  will not find the module if the variable is false, but the code will still be dependent on the module if the environmental variable is set to true later.  Another simple example of this is the DBI module.  It will not load a given DBD module until a connect is called.  Consider my $dbh = DBI->connect(@ENV{qw); What DBD module does this code require?  The problem here is that you cannot be certain all of the modules for a script will be loaded at the start (or any other time for that matter) of a script if the author has decided to load them dynamically.  Hopefully the author has documented this well, but no autodetection code can be guaranteed to find it.  You might also want to look into the Halting problem* if you don't believe me.  If you solve it you can make a lot of money. * http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halting_problem --  Chas. Owens wonkden.net The most important skill a programmer can have is the ability to read. --blcobidaiaohdgilcjej Content-Type: message/rfc822 Content-Disposition: inline; filename="beginners_98815.ezm" >From uiaregi@gunnar.cc  Sun Feb 24 10:31:21 2008 Message-ID: <20080219033206.22962.qmail@lists.develooper.com> To: soiofdvxg@perl.org Date: Tue, 19 Feb 2008 04:34:00 +0100 From: Gunnar Hjalmarsson  MIME-Version: 1.0 Subject: Re: Dependancies Content-Type: text/plain; charset=ISO-8859-1; format=flowed Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit Okay, thanks, now I understand what you mean. Chas. Owens wrote: > ... to detect what dependencies the code has Even if dependencies is the subject of this thread, it's not really what  the OP asked about. He simply asked for an "easy way to get a list of  all modules loaded by a Perl script". A module that isn't always loaded may still be considered a dependency.  So I have a feeling that we are comparing apples with oranges... --  Gunnar Hjalmarsson Email: http://www.gunnar.cc/cgi-bin/contact.pl --blcobidaiaohdgilcjej Content-Type: message/rfc822 Content-Disposition: inline; filename="beginners_98817.ezm" >From ahlg.rwhsv@gmail.com  Sun Feb 24 10:31:21 2008 Message-ID: <58ce48dc0802181953u750a0185qf544e0c3d202627f@mail.gmail.com> Date: Mon, 18 Feb 2008 22:53:25 -0500 From: "Chas. Owens"  To: "Gunnar Hjalmarsson"  Subject: Re: Dependancies Cc: soiofdvxg@perl.org MIME-Version: 1.0 Content-Type: text/plain; charset=ISO-8859-1 Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit On Feb 18, 2008 10:34 PM, Gunnar Hjalmarsson  wrote: > Okay, thanks, now I understand what you mean. > > Chas. Owens wrote: > > ... to detect what dependencies the code has > > Even if dependencies is the subject of this thread, it's not really what > the OP asked about. He simply asked for an "easy way to get a list of > all modules loaded by a Perl script". > > A module that isn't always loaded may still be considered a dependency. > So I have a feeling that we are comparing apples with oranges... snip So long as you add the caveat that the detection code tells you what modules are currently loaded (i.e. not all modules that may be loaded by the script), I agree. --  Chas. body: Send Wekalist mailing list submissions to 	iuxxdkqk@list.scms.waikato.ac.nz To subscribe or unsubscribe via the World Wide Web, visit 	https://list.scms.waikato.ac.nz/mailman/listinfo/wekalist or, via email, send a message with subject or body 'help' to 	ohwwlejs-pbfotbp@list.scms.waikato.ac.nz You can reach the person managing the list at 	pobypuhs-hvhzj@list.scms.waikato.ac.nz When replying, please edit your Subject line so it is more specific than "Re: Contents of Wekalist digest..." Today's Topics:    1. Weka  Weight Adjustment problem (Deepak Kumar)    2. unsubstribe weka messages (byjufkgul@163.com)    3. Re: unsubstribe weka messages (Luc Sorel)    4. WEKA and PMML (Peter SchÃ¼ffler)    5. question on feature selection (FANG WANG)    6. Re: data mining web service using weka (Omar Jasso) ---------------------------------------------------------------------- >From cluuis.banfv@globallogic.com  Sun Feb 24 10:30:39 2008 Message: 1 Date: Fri, 15 Feb 2008 18:11:48 +0530 From: "Deepak Kumar"  Subject: [Wekalist] Weka  Weight Adjustment problem To:  Message-ID: <0b72919600005801@globallogic.com> Content-Type: text/plain;	charset="us-ascii"   I have a set of records. Each of them has a given result value that is either true or false. Now each record has a table of values with fixed Number of columns. Now each column has certain weightage. So in the records below, C1 has weigh of 1 and C2 has weight of 2. Record 1 has Value 'True' Record 2 has Value 'False So total score for record 1 (Where C1= 10, and C2 = 5) would be 20 (C1*W1 + C2*W2 = 10*1+5*2) So total score for record 2 (Where C1= 5, and C2 = 10) would be 25 (C1*W1 + C2*W2 = 5*1+10*2) Now we want to calculate/optimize the values of weights W1 and W2 such that Record with value True has Higher Scores than Records With Value False for most of the records. So for the above example if we swap the weights for the column, we'll get the desired results. Before Swapping:     C1, C2       1, 2 R1: 10, 5 -> 20 R2: 5, 10 -> 25 After Swaping:     C1, C2       2, 1 R1: 10, 5 -> 25 R2: 5, 10 -> 20 Now Record R1 with Value 'True' has higher score than Record R2 with Value false. Is there any way in Weka So that we can optimize the Value of these weights when we are given following details for each line: 1. Column Values C1, C2 2. Result Value True|false After calculating and Applying the weights the Total Score TS = (C1*W1 + C2*W2) should be greater for records with Value "True" than for Value "False" In most of the cases. Regards Deepak ------------------------------ >From byjufkgul@163.com  Sun Feb 24 10:30:39 2008 Message: 2 Date: Fri, 15 Feb 2008 21:05:55 +0800 (CST) From: byjufkgul@163.com Subject: [Wekalist] unsubstribe weka messages To: "Weka machine learning workbench list." 	 Message-ID: 	<3999459.061642225399945906.xgkwioft.lblinjsi@bj163app87.163.com> Content-Type: text/plain; charset="gbk"        I want to unsubscribe weka messages ,but I do not know how to do it . could you do it for me. Thank you advanced. -------------- next part -------------- An HTML attachment was scrubbed... URL: https://list.scms.waikato.ac.nz/pipermail/wekalist/attachments/20080215/9db0409a/attachment-0001.html ------------------------------ >From vrdfs@agrocampus-rennes.fr  Sun Feb 24 10:30:39 2008 Message: 3 Date: Fri, 15 Feb 2008 14:55:49 +0100 From: Luc Sorel  Subject: Re: [Wekalist] unsubstribe weka messages To: "Weka machine learning workbench list." 	 Message-ID: <47B599E5.1060201@agrocampus-rennes.fr> Content-Type: text/plain; charset="x-gbk" Hello, byjufkgul@163.com a Â¨Â¦crit: > I want to unsubscribe weka messages ,but I do not know how to do it .  > could you do it for me. Thank you advanced. Follow the link automatically added at the end of each posted message to  manage your subscription  (https://list.scms.waikato.ac.nz/mailman/listinfo/wekalist). It leads to  the Weka mailing list information page. At the bottom of this page,  there is a form which you can use to unsubscribe (see "/To unsubscribe  from Wekalist, get a password reminder, or change your subscription  options enter your subscription email address:/"). Cordially, --  Luc Sorel Chercheur doctorant / PhD Candidate Agrocampus - INRA Rennes, UMR SAS 65 rue de Saint Brieuc - CS 84215 35042 Rennes Cedex, France Phone: 02 23 48 59 42 (from abroad +33 223 48 59 42) Fax: 02 23 48 54 30 (from abroad +33 223 48 54 30) 	Luc Sorel's website  	Centre Inra de Rennes  	Agrocampus Rennes  	Professional profile on LinkedIn  -------------- next part -------------- Skipped content of type multipart/related ------------------------------ >From yecrhvkk@mpi-sb.mpg.de  Sun Feb 24 10:30:39 2008 Message: 4 Date: Fri, 15 Feb 2008 15:00:38 +0100 From: Peter SchÃ¼ffler  Subject: [Wekalist] WEKA and PMML To: "Weka machine learning workbench list." 	 Message-ID: <47B59B06.50706@mpi-sb.mpg.de> Content-Type: text/plain; charset=ISO-8859-1; format=flowed Hi, I'am working on a project that generates Logistic Regression Models with  the Logistic function of WEKA. Now, I'm looking for a method that can convert the resulting model in  PMML (xml based Model language). Is there meanwhile a class or tool that can do this? Or has anyone  experience with it? Thank you! Peter ------------------------------ >From fueae@CLEMSON.EDU  Sun Feb 24 10:30:39 2008 Message: 5 Date: Fri, 15 Feb 2008 18:37:24 -0500 (EST) From: "FANG WANG"  Subject: [Wekalist] question on feature selection To: iuxxdkqk@list.scms.waikato.ac.nz Message-ID: <4544.130.127.150.224.1203118644.squirrel@wm.clemson.edu> Content-Type: text/plain;charset=iso-8859-1 Hello All: I am recently doing feature selection and classification. For example, I have a gene list, each gene contains 15 features, With Weka, by using filter, I get 5 features among the initial 15 fetures, with these 5 features, using supervised classifer, I could seperate these genes into cancer-related and noncancer-related, Now I want to use this 5 features to scan another group of genes, and want to try to find out which is cancer related and which is not. In a brief sentence, my question is: 1.a list of genes (each gene has 15 features, I already know which is cancer related and which is  not) 2.a list of genes to be investigate on. (each gene have the same feature as above genes, but no idea of which is cancer related genes) Any suggestion and idea on this question will be so appreciated. Thanks! Fang Wang ------------------------------ >From gdqoxq@xyzzy.claranet.de  Sun Feb 24 10:30:11 2008 Message: 2 Date: Tue, 12 Feb 2008 21:37:54 +0100 From: "Frank Ellermann"  Subject: [ietf-dkim] Re: ISSUE:  Rename SSP to ASP To: gfky-hddl@mipassoc.org Message-ID:  Content-Type: text/plain;	charset "Hallam-Baker, Phillip"  schrieb [...] Intro intentionally left as my MUA presents it in a reply. > My problem here is that Phill Hallam-Baker is the Author > of this email but verisign.com would be the signer. Actually somebody using the address  is the purported author, and that person or entity picked "Hallam-Baker, Phillip" as pretty name.  IMO versisign.com could be in a position to judge whether they sign the mail. At least they know that they have a user with the mailbox "pbaker".  Maybe they don't know if the mail was submitted by Phill Hallam-Baker, or by somebody else writing mails in his name on a box permanently or temporarily attached to their networks.  But DKIM clearly says that it is *not* supposed to replace (or emulate) S/MIME and OpenPGP.   The 2822 term  has the function to identify the author(s), there is no guarantee that it is correct, and DKIM also does not offer this guarantee.  SSP only allows to reject any unsigned 2822-From:   IFF the relevant verisign.com admin published a statement that they always sign @verisign.com and consider unsigned versions as broken or fakes. That includes valid mails from the real Phill via another ESP with Sender:  and the SSP-protected It would be also the moment where I'd ask this relevant admin if she's on crack or something, but depending on the domain I might settle to use a Reply-To for my favourite address with From:  Apparently this WG doesn't see the point in PRA and 2822, now they use "author", neither "sender" nor PRA  Let's see what happens... > I would be somewhat nervous about making the assertion > that a domain is the author of a message. That might be > exploited for purposes of legal mumbo-jumbo. The domain signs mails of authors using From-addresses in this domain, and it is free to assert this "ASP", it does not say that it "is the author".  Actually domains do not "sign" or "say" anything, that is already an abstraction. But "domain is the author" clearly make no sense.  IANAL, if you are worried add an explanation to the "overview".  Frank ------------------------------ >From gdtqzd@cinvestav.mx  Sun Feb 24 10:30:39 2008 Message: 6 Date: Fri, 15 Feb 2008 19:00:36 -0600 From: Omar Jasso  Subject: [Wekalist] Re: data mining web service using weka To: iuxxdkqk@list.scms.waikato.ac.nz Message-ID: <47B635B4.5080003@cinvestav.mx> Content-Type: text/plain; charset=UTF-8; format=flowed Hello everyone Some weeks ago I wrote asking for help to resolve a problem. I'm building  a data mining web service, I mean, like a weka's  middleware for the web It uses classes located into weka.jar file The problem was my web service seems not find the mysql connector driver I had modified DatabaseUtils.props for the correct mysql driver and url. Also I had located weka and mysql connector jar files into WEB-INF/lib  directory of Axis Now, I've gotten a solution. I realized the Utils.java class, located in /weka/core/ directory, it  wasn't loading (or locating) the DatabaseUtils.props file when is used  in Web Applications (like JSP or Web Service) /public static Properties readProperties(String resourceName)  throws  Exception { //Properties defaultProps = new Properties();     try {       // Apparently hardcoded slashes are OK here       // jdk1.1/docs/guide/misc/resources.html        defaultProps.load(ClassLoader.getSystemResourceAsStream(resourceName));     } catch (Exception ex) { /*      throw new Exception("Problem reading default properties: "     + ex.getMessage()); */       System.err.println("Warning, unable to load properties file from "              +"system resource (Utils.java)");     }     . . . . .    . . . . . /I resolved it locating the DatabaseUtils.props in my "home" directory.  I'm working in Ubuntu 7.10 Trying another different way I've set a environment variable (e.g.  "WEKA_HOME"), and into its path I've located the DatabaseUtils.props file. Then I wrote the next code in /readProperties/ method / / /Properties wekaHomeProps = new Properties(userProps);     propFile = new File(System.getenv("WEKA_HOME") + File.separatorChar  + resourceName);         if(propFile.exists()){         try{             wekaHomeProps.load(new FileInputStream(propFile));         } catch (Exception ex){             throw new Exception("Problem reading WEKA_HOME properties: "  + propFile);         }     } /And now it's working by either two ways Greetings/ / --  Jorge Omar Jasso L. Cinvestav - Tamaulipas Laboratorio de TecnologÃÂ­as de InformaciÃÂ³n Km. 6 carretera Cd. Victoria-Monterrey C.P. 87276 Cd. Victoria, Tam. subject: Re: svn commit: r586641 - /spamassassin/trunk/lib/Mail/SpamAssassin/Message/Node.pm
body:
Michael Parker writes:
> Mark Martinec wrote:
> >>> +use Encode;
> >> indicates that Encode is not available on perl 5.6.1.
> > 
> > ... but its routines are already used in Message/Node.pm !?
> > 
> > Mark
> > 
> 
> Aren't they wrapped around a conditional?  -1 for requiring Encode.
> We've had this discussion before.

Yep, -1 on that change from me too.  It needs to be reverted asap.

Please test with perl 5.6.1 before committing... 5.6.1 and 5.8.x have
radically different mechanisms to deal with UTF-8.

--j.

subject: Re: [Python-Dev] r62129 - in python/trunk: PCbuild/debug.vsprops	PCbuild/kill_python.c PCbuild/kill_python.vcproj PCbuild/pcbuild.sln	PCbuild/pythoncore.vcproj PCbuild/release.vsprops	Tools/buildbot/Makefile	Tools/buildbot/build-amd64.bat Tools/buildbo...
body:
Ok, I'll change the approach this weekend.

    Trent.

________________________________________
From: "Martin v. LÃ¶wis" [qpnysl@v.loewis.de]
Sent: 04 April 2008 19:57
To: Trent Nelson
Cc: Christian Heimes; zvllln-eum@python.org
Subject: Re: [Python-Dev] r62129 - in python/trunk: PCbuild/debug.vsprops PCbuild/kill_python.c PCbuild/kill_python.vcproj PCbuild/pcbuild.sln PCbuild/pythoncore.vcproj PCbuild/release.vsprops Tools/buildbot/Makefile Tools/buildbot/build-amd64.bat Tools/buildbo...

> What do others that do Windows development think?  I don't have a
> problem changing the build behaviour if the approach I've taken is
> generally disliked.

I think kill_python should only ever be invoked in the build slaves;
it should *not* be part of the regular build. If developers find they
can't build because some files are still open, they should kill
the processes themselves.

Regards,
Martin

subject: Re: [UAI] Is it luck or is it skill?
body:
peter wrote:
> I don't know much about Warren Buffet but I (believe I)
> do know this much: he studies the companies and
> enterprises he invests in at great length and
> with great care. (Many people say that he knows the
> enterprises he invests in better than do the insiders
> who run those enterprises.) This characteristic of
> diligent & intelligent study - or, alternatively, of more
> diligent and more intelligent study -- differentiates
> Buffet from some other investment analysts.


This is a carefully crafted marketing and public relations image,
basically a very slick image campaign to make him look like a Regular
Joe hard-working investor.  For people that work on the inside of big
business finance and get to see the sausage being made nothing could be
further from the truth.

The short story is that he is *extremely* well connected and has the
inside track on many sweetheart deals.  That is where he makes the
money.  The "Sage of Omaha" has the aura of a studious stock picker, but
that is mostly for show and he exploits the mythos to the hilt.  He is
no different than any other successful big finance guy, he just uses
novel brand identity.


j. andrew rogers

subject: Re: selective greylisting based on user preference
body:
mouss wrote:
> Dennis Trabjerg wrote:
>> Hi all.
>>
>> I run the companyÂ´s mailserver and would like to be able to implement 
>> greylisting on selected mailaccounts.
>>
>> Users should be able to choose greylisting as an option per. username.
>>
>> So basically i need postfix to do a quick lookup in a MySQL table to 
>> see if the username has opted for greylisting and then, if yes, 
>> greylist the mail via either postgrey or SQLgrey.
>>
>> How would you guys (if possible and reasonable) go about implementing 
>> such a solution ?
>>
>> Thanks.
>> /Dennis
>>
>
> smtpd_recipient_restrictions =
>    ...
>    reject_unauth_destination
>    ...
>    check_recipient_access mysql:$dir/per_recipient_greylist.cf
>    ..
>
> when per_recipient_greylist.cf returns
>    check_policy_service ....
> if the recipient wants greylisting and either returns DUNNO or does 
> not match if the recipient doesn't want greylisting. something like:
>
> query = select 'check_policy_service ...' where '%s' = user and 
> greylist='yes'
>
> should do.
Thank you for competent answer. -Seems to be working as intended.

-Implemented /etc/postfix/per_recipient_greylist.cf with the following;
hosts = my.mysql.server
user = postfix_mysql_username
password = postfix_mysql_password
dbname = postfix_database
table = postfix_usernames
select_field = policy_service
where_field = email_address

-Modified my postfix_usernames table to contain a column 
"policy_service" containing either "check_service" to enable greylisting 
or "OK" to bypass greylisting.

-Added a restriction class called "check_greylist"
smtpd_restriction_classes = check_greylist
check_greylist = check_policy_service inet:127.0.0.1:60000

And added per_recipient_greylist.cf to my recipient restrictions. in 
main.cf

Now im just wondering what will happen if the MySQL query returns 
something else than OK or check_greylist ?

Also curious to try this approach with user-preference-based amavisd-new 
and policy-d services. -Any comments on that issue ?

For now i have only implemented this solution on a low-volume backup-mx, 
and tailing the logs at the moment. any concers on using the above setup 
in a production enviroment ?

Thanks!

/Dennis

subject: [UAI] reminder: call for papers (Special issue of Neural Networks)
body: *************************  R E M I N D E R  ************************** 		    C A L L   F O R   P A P E R S 		   Special Issue of Neural Networks 				  on       Neural Networks and Kernel Methods for Structured Domains     Co-editors:Barbara Hammer, Craig Saunders, Alessandro Sperduti      (http://www.informatik.uni-osnabrueck.de/barbara/specialNN/)       >>>>>     Deadline for submission    October 11, 2004 <<<<< - ---------------------------------------------------------------------- In  recent years  several  researchers have  started  to consider  the adaptive processing of structured  data. This interest is motivated by two main reasons: i)  several very important  computational problems  in Bioinformatics, Chemistry, document classification and filtering (just to name a few), require  the use  of some  Machine Learning  procedure to  be properly treated because their  complexity does not allow a  formal and precise definition  of the  problem and  thus no  algorithmic solution  to the problem is known; on the other hand experimental data is available and usable by a Machine Learning approach; ii) in  many of the above  problems, the objects of  interest are more naturally  represented  via  varying-size structured  representations, such  as sequences,  strings,  trees, directed  or undirected  graphs. Moreover,  it  is commonly  believed  that  any computational  process representing   these    objects   via   flat    fixed-size   vectorial representations, risks to  discard structural information relevant for solving the task at hand. The  aim  of the  special  issue is  to  bring  together recent  works developed  mainly into the  fields of  Recurrent and  Recursive Neural Networks,  and  Kernel  Methods,  with  the  hope  to  function  as  a conceptual support for the improvement of current technologies and the growth  of new  integrated  methodologies for  learning in  structured domains. TOPICS: Recurrent  and  Recursive Neural  Networks,  Self-Organizing Maps  for Structures, Support Vector Machines  and Kernel Methods for Structured Domains, Fisher Kernels,  Probabilistic Models for Structured Domains, Applications  of  the  above  methods  to  Structured  Domains,  other relevant topics. - ---------------------------------------------------------------------- 		    I M P O R T A N T   D A T E S            Deadline for submission    October 11, 2004            Notification of acceptance:  March 14, 2005            Deadline for final paper:      June 6, 2005 - ---------------------------------------------------------------------- 		   P A P E R   S U B M I S S I O N Format: http://www.informatik.uni-osnabrueck.de/barbara/specialNN/ Papers must be submitted in electronic form (either Postscript or PDF) to the following 	  e-mail address: zokedzvwj.tqecfwhzo@math.unipd.it Hard-paper submissions  are accepted only  if the authors do  not have access to electronic facilities, and should be mailed to the following address: Prof. Alessandro Sperduti Dipartimento di Matematica Pura ed Applicata Via Belzoni 7 35131 Padova ITALY - ----------------------------------------------------------------------

subject: Compiling Perl for Windows Mobile (wince)
body:
Hello all,

I am trying to get Perl working on Windows Mobile 2005. I found some
binaries on the 'net, but they don't work on the device; it complains
about them not being signed with a trusted certificate. Presumably,
this can be fixed by compiling with Microsoft Visual Studio 2005; that
approach worked for a "hello world" program I wrote.

So I'm trying to compile Perl using Visual Studio 2005. I've tried
both 5.8.8 and 5.9.5, without success. I'll describe the process using
5.9.5 here, as, with that one, I at least managed to get Perl compiled
for win32.

I've obtained perl-5.9.5 source code from perl.org and celib-3.11-dll
and console-1.3 source from wince-devel.org.

Extracted all three in D:\\projex\\perlce.

Following the instructions in README.ce (found at
http://search.cpan.org/~rgarcia/perl/README.ce; curiously, it seems to
be missing from my tarball, downloaded from perl.org):

1. Went into D:\\projex\\perlce\\perl-5.9.5\\win32
2. Edited ce-helpers\\compile.bat, as follows (all lines not mentioned
   here are unchanged):

   set ARG-3=INSTALL_ROOT=\\perl
   set ARG-6=CELIBDLLDIR=d:\\projex\\perlce\\celib-palm-3.0
   set ARG-7=CECONSOLEDIR=d:\\projex\\perlce\\w32console
   rem set ARG-8=YES=/y
   set ARG-10=MACHINE=wince-arm-pocket-wce300
   set ARG-12=MSVCDIR=C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Visual Studio 8\\VC

3. Ran compile.bat. This resulted in an error: 'nmake' is not
   recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or
   batch file.

This was to be expected, as I hadn't set the environment
variables. README.ce doesn't instruct me to, but I found out this is
necessary for getting pretty much anything using Visual Studio to
work.

4. Ran "C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Visual Studio 8\\VC\\bin\\vsvars32.bat".

5. Ran compile.bat again. This resulted in an error message about
   windows.h not being found:

        nmake -nologo -f Makefile "CCHOME=C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Visual Stud
io 8\\VC" "CCINCDIR=C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Visual Studio 8\\VC\\include" "CCLIB
DIR=C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Visual Studio 8\\VC\\lib" "INCLUDE=C:\\Program Files
\\Microsoft Visual Studio 8\\VC\\include" "LIB=C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Visual St
udio 8\\VC\\lib" "LINK_FLAGS=" .\\config.h ..\\miniperl.exe
        del /f config.h
Could Not Find D:\\projex\\perlce\\PERL-5~1.5\\win32\\config.h
        copy config_H.vc config.h
        1 file(s) copied.
        if not exist ".\\mini" mkdir ".\\mini"
        cl -c -nologo -GF -W3 -I..\\lib\\CORE -I.\\include -I. -I.. -DWIN32 -D_CONS
OLE -DNO_STRICT -DHAVE_DES_FCRYPT -DPERLDLL -DPERL_CORE   -MD -Zi -DNDEBUG -O1 -
DPERL_EXTERNAL_GLOB -Fo.\\mini\\av.obj ..\\av.c
av.c
D:\\projex\\perlce\\PERL-5~1.5\\win32\\win32.h(59) : fatal error C1083: Cannot open i
nclude file: 'windows.h': No such file or directory
NMAKE : fatal error U1077: '"C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Visual Studio 8\\VC\\bin\\c
l.EXE"' : return code '0x2'
Stop.
NMAKE : fatal error U1077: '"C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Visual Studio 8\\VC\\bin\\n
make.EXE"' : return code '0x2'
Stop.

6. I looked for windows.h and found it in C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft
   Visual Studio 8\\VC\\PlatformSDK\\Include, among other places. I
   decided that the one in PlatformSDK was probably the right one,
   because the other ones relate to Windows Mobile, and we are looking
   at a compilation of miniperl here, which, if I understand
   correctly, should be built for the host platform, not the target
   platform.

   Doing some further digging, I found it is defining INCLUDE based on
   what I had specified as MSVCDIR in compile.bat. Presumably, cl.exe
   is looking for windows.h only in %MSVCDIR%\\include. So, if I want
   it to find windows.h, I should probably set %MSVCDIR% to C:\\Program
   Files\\Microsoft Visual Studio 8\\VC\\PlatformSDK.

7. Changed MSVCDIR in compile.bat to C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Visual
   Studio 8\\VC\\PlatformSDK, and ran compile.bat again. This time, it
   couldn't find sys/types.h.

        nmake -nologo -f Makefile "CCHOME=C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Visual Stud
io 8\\VC\\PlatformSDK" "CCINCDIR=C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Visual Studio 8\\VC\\Pla
tformSDK\\include" "CCLIBDIR=C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Visual Studio 8\\VC\\Platfo
rmSDK\\lib" "INCLUDE=C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Visual Studio 8\\VC\\PlatformSDK\\in
clude" "LIB=C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Visual Studio 8\\VC\\PlatformSDK\\lib" "LINK
_FLAGS=" .\\config.h ..\\miniperl.exe
'.\\config.h' is up-to-date
        cl -c -nologo -GF -W3 -I..\\lib\\CORE -I.\\include -I. -I.. -DWIN32 -D_CONS
OLE -DNO_STRICT -DHAVE_DES_FCRYPT -DPERLDLL -DPERL_CORE   -MD -Zi -DNDEBUG -O1 -
DPERL_EXTERNAL_GLOB -Fo.\\mini\\av.obj ..\\av.c
av.c
d:\\projex\\perlce\\perl-5.9.5\\perl.h(587) : fatal error C1083: Cannot open include
 file: 'sys/types.h': No such file or directory
NMAKE : fatal error U1077: '"C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Visual Studio 8\\VC\\BIN\\c
l.EXE"' : return code '0x2'
Stop.
NMAKE : fatal error U1077: '"C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Visual Studio 8\\VC\\BIN\\n
make.EXE"' : return code '0x2'
Stop.

  (Hey, that's a Unix header file, isn't it? But I think it is
  provided on Windows as well, for compatibility with BSD Sockets,
  among other things.)

  Looking for sys/types.h turned up various locations, including
  C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Visual Studio 8\\VC\\Include and some
  locations relating to Windows Mobile which, again, I assume aren't
  the ones we need here.

  So, basically, if I specify ...\\VC as %MSVCDIR%, the compiler can
  find sys/types.h, but can't find windows.h, and if I specify
  ...\\VC\\PlatformSDK, it can find windows.h but not
  sys/types.h. Catch!

8. Still on the idea that all we need from the native build is
   miniperl, and assuming that miniperl would be built as part of a
   native win32 build, I performed a native win32 build. Edited
   Makefile to have

   CCTYPE         = MSVC80

   and running nmake did the trick. Kudos, building Perl 5.9.5 for
   win32 is really easy!  (This contrary to 5.8.8, which I tried
   earlier, but couldn't get compiled for either wince or win32).

9. With miniperl built and supposedly working (I have no idea what
   it's capabilities are, but it grokked print "Hello\\n";), I gave
   compile.bat another shot, with MSVCDIR set to MSVCDIR=C:\\Program
   Files\\Microsoft Visual Studio 8\\VC once more.

   This time I got a complaint about xconfig.h not existing, and a
   failure to create it, because Cross.pm didn't exist:

Compiling for wince-arm-pocket-wce300
LIB=\\WCE300\\MS Pocket PC\\lib\\ARM
INCLUDE=\\WCE300\\MS Pocket PC\\include
PATH=

        del /f xconfig.h
Could Not Find D:\\projex\\perlce\\PERL-5~1.5\\win32\\xconfig.h
        mkdir ..\\xlib\\wince-arm-pocket-wce300\\CORE
        D:\\projex\\perlce\\PERL-5~1.5\\win32\\..\\miniperl.exe -I..\\lib -MCross=wince
-arm-pocket-wce300 config_h.PL "INST_VER=" "CORE_DIR=..\\xlib\\wince-arm-pocket-wc
e300\\CORE" "CONFIG_H=xconfig.h"
Can't locate Cross.pm in @INC (@INC contains: ..\\lib D:/projex/perlce/PERL-5~1.5
/lib .).
BEGIN failed--compilation aborted.
        xcopy  /f /r /i /d xconfig.h ..\\xlib\\wince-arm-pocket-wce300\\CORE\\config
h
File not found - xconfig.h
0 File(s) copied
NMAKE : fatal error U1077: 'C:\\WINDOWS\\system32\\xcopy.EXE' : return code '0x4'
Stop.

At this point, I decided to ask for help. I don't even know if I am on
the right track, because I've performed quite a lot of work that isn't
described in README.ce.  Perhaps the build is failing because I messed
up something. But then again, the instructions in README.ce apparently
aren't sufficient to get a working build for wince.

Can anybody help me along? Has anybody even tried to build Perl for
wince in recent times (the latest binaries I could find on the 'net
are pretty old). Perhaps wince really isn't supported, despite code
for it being included in the distribution?

Any help appreciated.

-- 
Time flies like an arrow.
Fruit flies like a banana.

subject: Re: [Buildbot-devel] Licensing clarification
body:
-----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
Hash: SHA1


> On Thu, 4 Oct 2007 12:40:20 +0200
> "Gianluca Sforna"  wrote:
> 
> Hi,
> A new Fedora policy requires me to better specify the license of
> packages included in Fedora 8.

Good points.

Buildbot is currently distributed as GPLv2. I haven't had a chance to read
over v3.. I might switch to that in the future, but for now it's v2.
Unfortunately, the only mention of this fact has been a tiny note buried in
the README.

What I've just done:

* put a copy of GPLv2 in the source tree as COPYING

* enhance the note in the README to explicitly declare our GPLv2-ness, and
  point to the COPYING file


I'm less eager to add the boilerplate to every source file, mostly because I
find it distracting when reading source code, but I'll think about it.

> So, what I would need is an official statement from the copyright
> holder(s) about which version of the GPL the sources are covered (v1,
> v2, v2 or later, etc)

Please consider this (signed) message as just such an official statement.

thanks!
 -Brian

subject: [UAI] Call for Organizing ICLP 2007
body:
CALL    FOR    PROPOSALS

			ICLP 2007:
       23rd International Conference on Logic Programming

The International Conference on Logic Programming (ICLP) is the
premier conference in the field of logic programming.  The Association
for Logic Programming (ALP), the body that oversees ICLP and various
activities related to research in Logic Programming, is seeking
proposals for hosting and organizing of ICLP in 2007. Past 5 ICLPs
have been held in Sitges (Spain, 2005), St. Malo (France, 2004),
Mumbai (India, 2003), Copenhagen (Denmark, 2002), and Paphos (Cyprus,
2001). ICLP 2006 will be held as part of the Federated Logic
Conference in Seattle, WA, USA, in August 2006.

If you are interested in organizing ICLP in 2007, please send email to
Gopal Gupta, ALP Conference Coordinator, at ipdvk@utdallas.edu before
June 20, 2007.

More information on what is involved in organizing an ICLP and
guidelines on how to write a proposal for organizing an ICLP can be
found in ALP's Conference Policy document that can be obtained from:

   http://www.cs.kuleuven.ac.be/~dtai/projects/ALP/
